tweets.full_text:
Claude Codeが月$20のProプランから消えかけた件、Anthropicの本音が見えた

昨日Anthropicが新規ユーザーの2%を対象にClaude CodeをMaxプラン以上に制限するテストを実施

・Proの料金表からClaude Codeのチェックマークが消えた
・Max 5x $100/月以上でしか使えない設定に変更
・公式ドキュメントもProの記載を削除済みだった
・数時間後にミスだったと認めて復旧

表向きは2%のA/Bテストだったけど、ドキュメントまで書き換えてたってことは本気で検討してた可能性が高い

Claude Codeのコンピュートコストを考えると月$20じゃ採算が合わないのは明らかで、、Simon Willisonも指摘してたけど全体的にかなり混乱した対応だった

開発者ツールの価格設定は一度値上げすると戻せない。ここで慎重に引いたのは正しい判断だと思うけど、$100/月への移行は時間の問題かもしれない

tweets.full_text:
【どうやって作るのか】(2/5)

Claude Codeのターミナルで
日本語でこう頼むだけ。

「〇〇を生成するSkillを作ってください。

　入力：〇〇・〇〇・〇〇
　出力：〇〇のフォーマットで整理されたファイル

　条件：
　・毎回同じデザインで出力する
　・スマホでも読みやすい形にする」

コードの中身はわからなくていい。

「動くか確認する」だけでいい。

30分後には使えるSkillができてた。

tweets.full_text:
【Skillsって何なのか】(1/5)

Claude CodeのSkillsっていうのは、
一度作ったら繰り返し使える
専用の命令セットみたいなもの。

毎回プロンプトを考えなくていい。

要するにこういうことだと思う。

「テーマと対象者を入れたら
　毎回同じデザインで資料が出てくる」

「会議の内容を貼ったら
　毎回同じフォーマットで議事録が出てくる」

「業務の手順を入れたら
　毎回読みやすいマニュアルが出てくる」

一度設計してしまえば
あとはずっと使える。

tweets.full_text: The 'prototype vs ship' energy is the wrong question. Most ask 'how do I get feedback faster?' Real pattern: lock in ONE clarity first (what problem am I actually solving?), then prototype becomes iteration. Built 13 AI tools at ActorLab over 6+ months on ONE pattern: Claude Code + explicit specs + decision rules. 127 commits/month because the clarity lets tools compound. Shipping without clarity just means faster failure. Build the clarity first.

tweets.full_text:
how to take any app from zero users to $10k a month using AI influencers:

the typical technical founder can vibe code a full app in a weekend with Cursor or Claude Code but then gets completely stuck on distribution. the product is ready but getting people to actually see it is a completely different skill set..

but i genuinely don't think there has ever been an easier time to distribute your product using AI. you can vibe code an app and then vibe market it with Higgsfield Marketing Studio

here's exactly how to do it step by step:

> set up for US distribution

this works best if you're targeting the US market but ofc any market with demand works. the US has the highest CPMs and the highest purchasing power so this is typically where you want your content to reach.

get a NordVPN dedicated IP and set it to New York or California. buy a US SIM card on Amazon. factory reset your phone before setting anything up. this matters for the algorithm because TikTok and Instagram will serve content mostly based on where they think you're located.

> create your accounts

set up TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube Shorts accounts. you're going to run multiple AI influencer pages all promoting your product from different angles.

create account name variations like:

{companyname}.sarah
mp4.jamess
studywith.katie
james.{companyname}

each account is a different AI persona talking about your product. this way you're not relying on one account to do all the work. if one gets traction you scale it. if one gets banned you have others running.

> create your AI influencers and start generating content

paste your product link into Higgsfield Marketing Studio. it automatically scrapes Meta Ads Library and TikTok Creative Center to find what's already working in your niche. the hooks that are getting engagement, the formats that are converting, the angles competitors are using. you don't have to do this research manually.

from there you generate AI personas with realistic voices, and consistent personalities. these become your army of creators

Marketing Studio generates UGC videos where your AI creator is actually holding and showing your product on screen. the screenshot stays legible, the UI doesn't warp between frames and the face stays consistent across every video. it actually looks like a real person filmed it..

the key is consistency. make sure to use the same face across every video on that account so it looks like a real person running the page. this is how you build trust with the audience.

> post volume and test everything

pump out 3-5 videos per day per account. test different hooks, different angles, different formats. the more you post the faster you figure out what resonates with your audience, data is key here.

track which videos drive clicks, which hooks get engagement, which accounts are growing fastest. kill what doesn't work and double down on what does.

the entire pipeline from idea to $10k MRR is now possible without hiring a single person. you can build the product with AI and distribute it with AI.

what a time to be alive..

tweets.full_text:
Those 7.7B tokens on Claude Opus 4.6 (API pricing: $5/M input, $25/M output) are worth $38k–$193k total depending on the input/output split. 

With prompt caching common in coding tools, real cost drops 80–90%—likely $5k–15k over 106 days. The Claude Code lead probably gets it free or heavily subsidized internally.

user.name: Chris Short
user.description: DevOps’ish | Kubernetes Contributor Comms | Open Source | Corgis | LEGO | Detroit | Disabled Veteran | Views solely mine

tweets.full_text:
What it covers:
- MCP servers (test, enable/disable, OAuth, inspect tools/resources/prompts)
- slash commands, hooks, permissions, agents, skills, memory
- usage tracking, cost charts
- session transcripts, plan history
- CC Bridge for Claude Code tmux sessions
- backup & restore

We built a self-hosted web dashboard for Claude Code.

Claude Deck gives you one place to manage MCP servers, slash commands, hooks, agents, usage, transcripts, and more — without digging through JSON files.

Built by me and my 13yo son Adrian.
https://t.co/eCgFfJfzcI

What Promptev MCP Server replaces:

- OAuth tokens expiring at 2am  
- "npm start" on 8 MCP servers every morning  
- New team members spending a day copying configs  
- Losing everything when switching Cursor → Claude Code  

One URL. Configure once. Use everywhere.

tweets.full_text:
This is one of the more useful MCP projects I’ve seen lately.

https://t.co/RsCDYzbnUz gives Claude Code semantic code search, so agents can retrieve the parts of a large codebase that actually matter instead of stuffing whole directories into the prompt. Better context quality, lower token cost, and a much more realistic way to work with big repos.

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code design principle I keep coming back to:  counterfactual verification.

Before any Skill, Routine, or architectural addition — ask what breaks if it doesn’t exist.

If nothing — don’t build it yet.
Precision over completeness. Every element should earn its place.

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code needs a /skill for “Edge Cases”.
If you don’t have one, make one. Your project’s feature have at least dozens of edge cases that u won’t think off and your “planmode” won’t too. Solution? Create a skill that will scan your complete plan, identifies edge-cases and returns back graded summary…makes your life and customer’s experience much better
